stages.objective: Observe read syscall block and IRQ wakeup path.
stages.theory_detail: Blocking read relinquishes CPU until device completion interrupt marks process runnable again.

hints.concept: I/O progress is asynchronous; wakeup is required before user execution resumes.
hints.explicit: Find trap.enter, io.submit, proc.wakeup, and trap.return in order.

stages.objective: Diagnose completion behavior for blocked read flow.
stages.goal: Pass wakeup trace and completion checks together.

hints.nudge: Do not stop after seeing wakeup once.
hints.concept: Wakeup must lead to resumed execution and eventual exit.
hints.explicit: Run additional steps until completion metric reaches target, then submit.

stages.objective: Tune latency while preserving read wakeup correctness.
stages.goal: Keep wakeup/order checks passing after config edits.

hints.nudge: Adjust latency one setting at a time.
hints.concept: Timing changes should not break control-flow correctness.
hints.explicit: Apply one latency change, rerun, and verify ordered wakeup path plus completion.
